primary.note: All 21 parts fully captured; 17-21 fetched on 2026-07-17 via the Chrome extension (URL hash #part-N/M + get_page_text).
primary.note2: updateBehavior was implicit until 2026-07-18 — the command's scope table described it, this file did not. A finite numbered series with no feed, so part-tracking is the only option; the check is whether a part 22 exists, not a re-read of 1-21.

secondary.note: All 8 fetched and extracted on 2026-07-18 (run 3) — the first run to give them a real extraction pass rather than treating them as a reference list. Heading markers recorded below so the next run can diff instead of re-extracting. Caveat learned this run: /en/settings renders truncated and does NOT list every key it owns — defaultMode, teammateMode, worktree.bgIsolation and includeGitInstructions are all real but documented on permission-modes, agent-teams, agent-view and sub-agents respectively. Never conclude a setting is nonexistent from that page alone.

living.note: Re-check each run. Change detection uses a stable `changeMarker`, never a content hash: WebFetch returns processed text that is not byte-stable, so a hash would fire on every run (same reason the primary source stores no raw hashes).

entries.note: CHANGED on 2026-07-18: gained 'Detect subagent invocation', 'Resume subagents' and 'Tool restrictions'. Those sections are what promoted the residual-"Task" and Windows-8191 claims from observation to documented contract.

Re-checked 2026-07-18: still 2.1.214, no newer entries. URL confirmed by the 2026-07-18 fetch (resolved, 51 bullet entries under the top version). Authoritative for WHEN something shipped, never for HOW it is used — entries not found in the docs go to 90-deprecated.md as changelog-only.

Fetch the raw README (raw.githubusercontent.com/anthropics/skills/main/README.md) rather than the HTML page. Content is a skill catalogue (docx/pdf/pptx/xlsx plus spec/template), not guidance — nothing ingested this run beyond confirming what it is.

secondaryFanMade.note: Mark anything taken from it as "(fan-made, Willison)"; never adopt a safety-/API-relevant claim without doc verification.
secondaryFanMade.discoveredVia: search result (title + URL + announcement; "first two chapters")
secondaryFanMade.dateNote: The guide page shows no date, but individual chapters do carry `created`/`last modified` — captured per chapter as `lastModified` since run 4. `announcementDate` belongs to `announcementUrl`, not to the guide; it was called `date` until 2026-07-18, and a citation in 50-verification.md carried it as though it were the chapter's date. Run 4 removed that date from the citation, so no KB line now dates a chapter from the announcement.
secondaryFanMade.chapterMarkerNote: `chapterCount` detects NEW chapters only, never an edit to an existing one — and this guide does revise chapters (several carry a `last modified` later than `created`). `lastModified` per chapter, captured on read, is the marker that catches revisions. Re-read a chapter whose `lastModified` has moved even if its status is `ingested`.

chapters.title: What is agentic engineering?

chapters.title: Writing code is cheap now
chapters.yield: none — philosophy on the economics of code, no setup mechanism

chapters.title: Hoard things you know how to do

chapters.title: AI should help us produce better code

chapters.title: Anti-patterns: things to avoid
chapters.yield: none — despite the title it holds ONE anti-pattern ('do not open a PR with unreviewed agent code'), which is code-review hygiene, not agent-setup guidance

chapters.title: How coding agents work

'Cheap to prompt' and 'fixes the acceptance criterion before any code exists' appear nowhere in the chapter. Rewritten to what it does say: confirm the test fails first; guards code-that-doesn't-work AND code-that-is-never-used; leaves a regression suite.

chapters.title: Agentic manual testing
chapters.yield: THE find of this run — a concrete loop for work with no automated test surface: start it, drive it, capture observable evidence, have the agent inspect the evidence, and record real command output so a result is shown rather than asserted. The KB had nothing on this.

secondaryFanMade.note2: CORRECTED 2026-07-18. Until this run the entry pointed at `announcementUrl` — the dated blog post that ANNOUNCES the guide, not the guide. A dated post is frozen by construction, so it reported "the first two chapters" forever and three runs concluded the guide had stalled. It had not: the guide is at 16 chapters. The earlier conclusion 'still at 2 chapters after ~5 months, consider demoting' was an artifact of polling the wrong URL, and a first repair that repointed this entry at the site's blog feed made it worse by abandoning the on-topic surface entirely. The guide has no feed and changes in place, so `check-every-run` + `chapterCount` is the right shape; the general blog feed is tracked separately under `feeds`.
secondaryFanMade.note3: RUN 4 (2026-07-18) read 14 of the 16 chapters; the 2 annotated-prompt walkthroughs were deliberately skipped and say so in their entries. Honest yield: ONE chapter carried something the KB did not have ('Agentic manual testing'), one produced a correction to an existing KB line ('Red/green TDD'), one raised a claim that turned out to be worth verifying though the chapter itself was background ('How coding agents work'). The other eleven yielded nothing. The pre-run expectation named five chapters as 'directly on-topic'; three of those five yielded nothing at all ('Subagents', 'Anti-patterns', 'First run the tests'), and only one of the two that did produced a KB addition. Recorded without a ratio: an earlier draft of this note claimed 'too optimistic by a factor of about five', which was a made-up figure caught in review. The usable lesson is the raw count, not a multiplier.

secondaryGuides.note: Long-form living guides that change in place. `wordCount` is the changeMarker: both pages print `words: N` and `updated: <date>` as page metadata — cheaper than a hash, more stable than headings (same reasoning as `topVersion`). Mark anything taken from them "(fan-made, blakecrosley)". Three claim classes, and only the first can enter as fact: Claude Code facts (verifiable, docs win), the author's own architecture (a pattern, never a fact about the product), industry/security news (no oracle in this KB's chain — contextOnly or omit). German `/de/` paths are OUT: translations, older and shorter than the originals.

entries.note: INCOMPLETE on purpose-of-record, not marked done. ~66.7k words across ~41 top-level sections; the fetch covered ~11 of them (config/env vars, model selection, interaction modes, permissions, hooks, subagents, partial MCP, memory, advanced patterns, background agents, best practices). CONFIRMED NOT FETCHED: Output Styles, Slash Commands reference, Skills, Plugins, multimodal/voice, Git/IDE/Chrome/Slack integrations, performance, debugging, enterprise deployment, migration guide, changelog. The remaining ~15 sections are UNACCOUNTED FOR — neither confirmed read nor confirmed skipped (among them Quick Start, How Claude Code Works, Cost, Decision Frameworks, Extended Thinking, Claude Code on the Web, Under the Hood, Keyboard Shortcuts, Workflow Recipes, Quick Reference). Correction after review: an earlier draft said 'the unfetched sections are listed', which overstated this bookkeeping. The next run must re-derive the section list from the page's own table of contents and diff it against what is named here — do not treat the not-fetched list as exhaustive.

Fetched complete. Mostly class 2 (the author's own architecture) — deliberation patterns, dissent-forcing, spawn budgets, the two-gate validation split. Those are patterns, not product facts, and were NOT ingested as claims this run; the KB already carries producer!=checker and fan-out guidance from doc-backed sources. The English original is 26,829 words / updated 2026-07-17, against the German translation's ~19,826 / 2026-07-07 — the two languages are NOT in sync, which is why the /de/ paths stay out of scope.

secondaryGuides.scopeNote: OUT: /writing/ai-engineering and /writing/agent-security. Both are index pages, not articles — the first fronts ~130 posts, which would mean carrying 130 items through every `full` run. Revisit only as a curated subset.

feeds.note: General-interest blogs that publish a stream of items. Tracked by feed, never by a fixed article URL — the mistake corrected in `secondaryFanMade` on 2026-07-18.

entries.watermarkNote: Deliberate one-off backfill window, set 2026-07-18. The obvious seed was 2026-07-18, but that would have made https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jul/15/claude-web-fetch-exfiltration/ — a prompt-injection/exfiltration writeup, and the very item that justified widening `relevance` to agent security — unreachable forever, because candidates are `>= watermark`. Backing the watermark to the 15th makes it a candidate. `seenIds` therefore holds two entries from a later day than the watermark, which is outside the normal invariant; the first successful run restores it (watermark = newest entry's date, seenIds = that date's permalinks).
entries.relevance: Claude Code, agent orchestration, subagents, hooks, skills, permissions, context management, prompt-injection/agent security. NOT general model releases, benchmarks, or unrelated tooling — those are the bulk of this feed.
entries.note: Same author as `secondaryFanMade`, different surface: ~20 posts/week, mostly off-topic for this KB. Low yield, not zero — an exfiltration/lethal-trifecta writeup sat just outside the window inspected on 2026-07-18, which is why `relevance` explicitly includes agent security. Mark anything taken from it as "(fan-made, Willison)".

contextOnly.note: Framing and orientation only — NOT part of the verification canon. Never adopt a claim from these without independent doc verification. Cite by source name when used.

entries.note: PDF — WebFetch often cannot read these. On failure record `incomplete` and fetch in the main run via the browser tool (see MAINTENANCE, known environment pitfalls).

openItems: Fan-made source fully captured (parts 1-21). Next run: only check for new parts.

UNRESOLVED (run 3): part 12's tab count read as 2 across two WebFetch passes against the stored 4. The page is JS-rendered and the same fetch was inconsistent on part numbering, so this is probably fetch noise, not a content change. Settle it with the browser tool in the main run rather than trusting WebFetch; nothing was written to the KB on the strength of it.

RESOLVED (run 4): the Willison guide was read, 14 of 16 chapters. The 'guide has stalled' conclusion of runs 1-3 is fully retired — it was an artifact of polling the announcement post, which is dated and therefore frozen. Before concluding a source is quiet, check that the URL is the source's living surface and not a dated post about it. Backlog is now 2 deliberately-skipped annotated-prompt chapters, both documented in place.

NEW (run 4): chapterCount detects new chapters but NOT edits to existing ones, and this guide revises chapters. Per-chapter lastModified is now stored on read; the next run must diff those, not just the count. Chapters whose lastModified moved need re-reading even at status ingested.

NEW (run 4): 10-context-memory.md carried `fork: true` as an experimental skill field while 90-deprecated.md had already refuted it in run 3 — a contradiction inside the KB that survived a run. Corrected to `context: fork`. Worth a consistency sweep for other claims refuted in one file but still asserted in another.

NEW (run 4): the ~300-400k context-rot figure in 10-context-memory.md is fan-made; the docs give the effect without any number. Left in place but marked, since the ~40% compaction recommendation rests on the effect rather than the figure.

NEW (run 5, found in review): TWO 'not documented' verdicts in this run were false, both caused by a truncated /en/env-vars fetch. This file has warned since run 3 that /en/settings renders truncated and does not list every key it owns — the lesson was recorded and then not applied one page over. Make it mechanical: a 'not documented' verdict requires either a complete fetch (state that it was complete) or confirmation from a second surface. Absence in a truncated page is not evidence.

NEW (run 5): the blakecrosley claude-code guide is only ~1/3 read and is marked `partial`, NOT done. The unfetched section list is in its `_state.json` note; continue from there rather than re-reading. This is the first source in this KB deliberately left half-ingested — the alternative was pretending a 66.7k-word page had been covered.

NEW (run 5): the second blakecrosley guide is mostly the AUTHOR'S OWN architecture (deliberation patterns, dissent-forcing, spawn budgets, two-gate validation). Nothing was ingested from that class this run. Open question for a later run: whether any of it is worth carrying as an explicitly-labelled pattern, given the KB already has producer!=checker and fan-out guidance from doc-backed sources. Do not ingest it as fact under any circumstances.

NEW (run 4, found in review): /reload-plugins was never tried when this repo diagnosed its mid-session plugin staleness. It is the documented in-session escape hatch, so 'start a fresh session' is one option, not a requirement. Anything in this repo's own planning docs that treats a new session as mandatory for picking up a plugin build overstates the constraint.

`contextOnly` (Trends Report PDF, InfoQ) still `pending` — deliberately, not an omission: its `updateBehavior` is `on-demand` and it may never serve as evidence for a claim. Fetch it only when framing is actually needed; the PDF will likely require the browser fallback.

The `managed-agents` docs page (platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/overview) was NOT fetched. The blog post carries no decision criteria, limits or pricing — if a managed-vs-local recommendation is ever needed, that page is the gap to close.

RESOLVED (run 3): the two observation-only claims parked in 90-deprecated.md — the residual `"Task"` name and the Windows 8191-character limit — are now documented verbatim in agent-sdk/subagents and have been promoted to contract. Worth remembering as a pattern: a claim that looks unsourced may simply be documented on a page the run did not fetch, so park rather than drop.

verified.note: Not an inventory of every verified line — only the contested claims a run had to settle. See INDEX.md.
confirmed: permissions.defaultMode is NESTED under permissions, not a top-level settings key; the project/local ignore rule applies only to the value `auto`, not to the key (run 3 correction).
permissions.ask exists alongside allow/deny; rules evaluate deny -> ask -> allow; ask rules still prompt under bypassPermissions and are denied under dontAsk.
teammateMode, worktree.bgIsolation and includeGitInstructions all exist — documented on agent-teams, agent-view and sub-agents respectively, NOT on the settings page.
Hook exit codes: only 2 blocks; exit 1 is non-blocking (contrary to Unix convention); on exit 2 stderr is what reaches Claude, stdout/JSON is ignored.
SubagentStart cannot block; SubagentStop can. Frontmatter Stop hooks auto-convert to SubagentStop in a subagent.
/agents stopped being a management panel in v2.1.198 (prints a pointer to .claude/agents/); distinct from `claude agents` (Agent View).
Explore inherits the main model since v2.1.198 (was always Haiku), capped at Opus on the Claude API; override with a project agent named Explore carrying model: haiku.
A parent's bypassPermissions/acceptEdits/auto overrides a subagent's own permissionMode — a subagent cannot be tightened below its parent.
Subagents are resumable via SendMessage without agent teams; Explore/Plan are one-shot and return no agent ID.
Windows 8191-character command-line limit on subagent prompts (documented, agent-sdk/subagents troubleshooting).
Residual `"Task"` in the system:init tools list and result.permission_denials[].tool_name after the v2.1.63 rename (documented; note the colon, not a slash).
Ultracode keyword fires only from genuine human input since v2.1.210 — not from -p, scheduled prompts, or webhook/PR-comment relays.
Workflow hard caps 16 concurrent / 1000 total per run; the /config 'Dynamic workflow size' guideline is advisory only.
CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW exists (default ~967k).
dontAsk is a valid permission mode (previously mismarked as invalid).
fable is an official alias = Claude Fable 5 (not speculative).
Effort low/medium/high/xhigh persist, max session-only; effortLevel setting only up to xhigh.
`claude --permission-mode auto` exists; `defaultMode: "auto"` is ignored from project/local settings.
Subagents run in the BACKGROUND by default since v2.1.198, and inherit the main session's extended thinking config — correcting the earlier fan-made claim that thinking weights do not propagate.
Nesting depth is fixed at 5 and not configurable (v2.1.172+); at depth 5 the Agent tool is simply withheld.
CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_SUBAGENTS_PER_SESSION (default 200, v2.1.212+).
Subagent output scanning v2.1.210+: backslash insertion into control-tag / `Human:` / `Assistant:` imitations, plus a `[harness: ...]` marker.
Skill limits: name <=64 chars (no 'anthropic'/'claude'), description <=1024, SKILL.md body <500 lines, `disable-model-invocation: true`.
cleanupPeriodDays defaults to 30 days; `--disable-slash-commands`, `--forward-subagent-text`, `claude auto-mode reset` all exist.
No Windows sandboxing — settling the fan-made 'coming soon' from Part 3 (Feb 2026) as still not shipped.
Built-in subagents are Explore, Plan and general-purpose (plus helper agents statusline-setup, claude-code-guide) — settled in run 4 against a fetcher that claimed no named built-ins exist.
CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_EXPLORE_PLAN_AGENTS=1 removes the Explore and Plan built-ins; a project agent named Explore overrides rather than supplements the built-in.

Prompt caching matches the prefix EXACTLY — any change in the prefix recomputes everything after it; there is no per-file or per-segment caching. This is the documented reason Claude Code appends rather than mutates: file re-reads append a <system-reminder>, skills/commands inject at the point of invocation, /recap appends where /compact replaces, /rewind truncates to a cached prefix, and CLAUDE.md edits do not take effect mid-session.
Claude Code on the web (claude.ai/code) exists as a documented surface in research preview; --cloud and --teleport move sessions between web and terminal.

/reload-plugins applies plugin changes (plugins, skills, agents, hooks, plugin MCP/LSP servers) to a RUNNING session without a restart; since v2.1.163 it warns and declines when the reload would force a full cache re-read (plugin with non-deferred MCP tools), with --force to override.

Marketplace plugins are copied to a per-version directory under ~/.claude/plugins/cache; an updated-away version is kept ~7 days as orphaned so running sessions keep working. THIS, not prompt caching, is why a running session keeps resolving an old plugin version.
Plugin-provided skills/commands/agents/hooks are APPENDED and never invalidate the prompt cache — the exception is a plugin providing non-deferred MCP tools, which follows the MCP rules.

autoMode holds `environment`, `allow`, `soft_deny`, `hard_deny` — arrays of PLAIN-LANGUAGE rules, with `$defaults` to extend rather than replace the built-in set. Distinct from permissions.allow/deny, which match tool patterns.
autoMode.classifyAllShell (default false) suspends every Bash and PowerShell allow rule so the classifier sees each one.

--safe-mode / CLAUDE_CODE_SAFE_MODE (v2.1.169+) starts with all customizations off (CLAUDE.md, skills, plugins, hooks, MCP, commands, agents, output styles, workflows, themes, keybindings, status line, LSP, auto-memory) while auth/model/built-in tools/permissions keep working — unlike --bare. Managed settings policy STILL applies, including policy-configured hooks, so it is not a full clean room.
fallbackModel takes an ARRAY tried in order, capped at three after dedup; the switch lasts the current turn only; /status does not display the chain. CLI --fallback-model wins over the setting.

disableBundledSkills / CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_BUNDLED_SKILLS disable the bundled skills and workflows (/doctor, /code-review, /batch, /debug, /loop, /claude-api). Since v2.1.205 /doctor stays typable regardless; hide it via DISABLE_DOCTOR_COMMAND or skillOverrides {"doctor": "off"}.

Prompt-caching env vars: DISABLE_PROMPT_CACHING plus per-model _HAIKU/_SONNET/_OPUS/_FABLE; ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1 for the 1h TTL, FORCE_PROMPT_CACHING_5M=1 to force 5m. Documented on /en/prompt-caching AND listed on /en/env-vars — an earlier run-5 draft claimed they were absent from the env-vars page, which was an artifact of a truncated fetch.

Auto mode availability is ALL PLANS (corrected 2026-07-18 from the KB's stale 'Max/Team/Enterprise'). Team/Enterprise additionally need an Owner to enable it in admin settings; permissions.disableAutoMode: "disable" in managed settings blocks it. Model/provider requirements apply separately.
Hook handler type `agent` is documented as EXPERIMENTAL; `http` hooks POST to a URL and are gated by the allowedHttpHookUrls setting.

A SendMessage relay is labelled as coming from another Claude session, not the user; a teammate denied an action cannot relay it to a second teammate to bypass the check (documented on /en/agent-teams#permissions; the guide's v2.1.166 attribution is NOT documented).
diverged: --tmux on the worktree does not exist (belongs to Agent Teams, not worktrees).
inherit is not a model alias, only a subagent-model value.
No documented '8 consecutive Stop-hook blocks' override — the 3-consecutive/20-total counters belong to Auto Mode's classifier fallback.
CLAUDE_CODE_OTEL_CONTENT_MAX_LENGTH: changelog-only; the 60 KB OTEL truncation is documented as fixed, with OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES as the escape hatch.
EndConversation: absent from the tools reference, so unusable in permission rules, tool lists or hook matchers.

`fork: true` does not exist on a skill or a subagent (fan-made Part 16 fragment). Real: SKILL frontmatter `context: fork` (+ optional `agent:`), and a separate `fork` subagent type invoked via /subtask (v2.1.212+, formerly /fork).
`disallowHooks` does not exist — the real key is `disableAllHooks`; enterprise-side control is allowManagedHooksOnly.
--tmux on the worktree: re-confirmed nonexistent on 2026-07-18; the string does not appear on the worktrees page at all.

'Quality degrades above ~200,000 tokens' (fan-made) — the context-rot effect is documented, the numeric threshold is not; 200k is a context-window size in the docs, so the figure is probably a conflation. Cite the effect, not the number.
'Claude Code for web' — the documented name is 'Claude Code on the web'.
Claude Opus 4.6 (claude-opus-4-6) is valid but LEGACY; current lineup is Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Sonnet 5, Haiku 4.5.

OUR OWN claim, refuted in review of run 4: 'prompt caching binds a plugin's skill path at session start'. The observation (a running session kept resolving the old version after `claude plugin update`) is real and reproducible, but the mechanism is the per-version plugin cache directory plus its 7-day orphan grace period. The docs say the opposite about caching: plugin skills are appended and never invalidate the prefix. Recorded because a correct observation with an invented mechanism reads exactly like a verified fact.

CLAUDE_CODE_WORKFLOWS=1 'enables the off-by-default Workflow tool' (fan-made, blakecrosley) — INVERTED. Workflows are on by default on paid plans; the real variable is CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_WORKFLOWS=1 / disableWorkflows. Version is v2.1.154, not v2.1.147.
Permission mode `delegate` (fan-made, blakecrosley) does not exist — the permission-modes page enumerates exactly six, and the list is exhaustive.

WITHDRAWN same day: 'SLASH_COMMAND_TOOL_CHAR_BUDGET is not documented'. It IS on /en/env-vars (overrides the skill-metadata char budget for the Skill tool; scales at 1% of the context window, 8,000-char fallback; legacy name). The guide was right and this run was wrong. Cause: /en/env-vars came back TRUNCATED on two separate fetches and absence-in-a-truncated-fetch was recorded as absence-in-the-docs — the same trap this file already documented for /en/settings in run 3. A truncated fetch can never establish a negative; re-fetch or use another page before writing any 'not documented' verdict.

'v2.1.166' as the version for the cross-session SendMessage authority rule (fan-made, blakecrosley) — the behaviour is documented, the version attribution is not. Same defect shape as run 4's plugin-caching error: a correct claim carrying an invented number.

note: tabs = observed tab count per part; the next run compares title/count to detect new/changed content. No raw-content hashes stored (the source only partially delivers content via WebFetch).
